data.title: Matador Resources expands credit deal
data.description:
• Matador Resources Company (NYSE:MTDR) has announced a $750M increase in borrowing base under its credit agreement to $3.25B.
  • This increase was unanimously supported by each of the 19 lenders under Matador’s credit facility. It resulted from the regularly scheduled semi-annual borrowing base redetermination, although Matador elected to keep the borrowing commitments at $2.25B at present but appreciates the potential to increase its borrowings to $3.25B at a later date.
  • As of Nov. 30, 2024, Matador had $830M outstanding under its revolving credit facility with approximately $1.4B in liquidity.
  • In addition, the company’s midstream joint venture, San Mateo Midstream, amended and restated its credit agreement to increase the lender commitments by ~50% from $535M to $800M and extend the maturity date to Nov. 2029.

data.title: China halts exports of gallium, germanium to U.S. in response to chip sanctions
data.description:
China has banned exports of "dual-use items" related to gallium, germanium, antimony and super hard materials to the U.S. with immediate effect, its commerce ministry said on Tuesday.

Beijing's directive on items with both military and civilian use, cites national security concerns, and comes a day after Washington's latest crackdown on China's chip sector.

"In principle, the export of gallium, germanium, antimony, and superhard materials to the United States shall not be permitted," the ministry said.

Gallium and germanium are used in semiconductors, while germanium is also used in infrared technology, fibre optic cables and solar cells.

China supplies 60% of the world’s germanium, prices of which hit record highs earlier this year in China.

President Xi Jinping’s government had last year placed the rare metals under stricter government oversight as tensions flared between the world’s largest economies.

President-elect Donald Trump has threatened new tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China - the top three trading partners of the U.S. - soon after he is sworn in. During the campaign trail, Trump had pledged to increase tariffs on Chinese imports by 60%-100%.

data.title: Dow names Karen Carter as COO
data.description:
• Dow (NYSE:DOW) said it has appointed Karen Carter as the COO, effective immediately.
  • Carter, who joined the firm in 1994, is currently president of Dow Packaging & Specialty Plastics.
  • She has held a broad progression of leadership positions in her more than 30-year career with Dow, including business operations, sales and marketing, and human resources.
  • Carter will also join the company's executive committee, and will continue to be based in Midland, Michigan.

data.title: Pentair acquires G&F Manufacturing for $108M in all-cash deal
data.description:
• Pentair (NYSE:PNR) has acquired Florida-based G&F Manufacturing, LLC for approximately $108 million in cash.
  • G&F Manufacturing, LLC manufactures and services heat pumps for pools under the Gulfstream brand in the Southeast portion of the United States.
  • The move expands Pentair's pool equipment portfolio in the Southeast United States and strengthens Pentair's position in sustainable pool solutions, particularly in critical geographical regions where heat pumps are essential offerings.
  • The deal is expected to be modestly accretive to Pentair earnings in 2025.

data.title: Dynamite Blockchain to acquire Kaspa Mining
data.description:
• Dynamite Blockchain (OTCPK:CRYBF) said it will acquire Kaspa Mining.
  • The deal price is expected to be 30M shares of the firm, at issue price of $0.16 apiece.
  • The acquisition is seen to boost the fimr's Kaspa-oriented capabilities.
  • Kaspa Mining currently owns and operates 25 high-performance Bitmain KS5 Pro Kaspa miners.
  • “Kaspa Mining brings the expertise, networks and infrastructure needed to propel our Kaspa focused operations to new heights,” said Akshay Sood, CEO of Dynamite.

data.title: Bank of Nova Scotia declares CAD 1.06 dividend
data.description:
• Bank of Nova Scotia (NYSE:BNS) declares CAD 1.06/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 5.5%
  • Payable Jan. 29; for shareholders of record Jan. 7; ex-div Jan. 7.
  • See BNS Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Bank of Nova Scotia Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.57, revenue of C$8.53B
data.description:
• Bank of Nova Scotia press release (NYSE:BNS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.57.
  • Revenue of C$8.53B (+2.6% Y/Y).
  • The provision for credit losses was $1,030 million, compared to $1,256 million, a decrease of $226 million. The provision for credit losses ratio decreased 11 basis points to 54 basis points.

data.title: Tesla's China shipments fall for second month in a row
data.description:
Sales of Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) China-made electric vehicles fell 4.3% year-on-year to 78,856 units in November, according to data from the China Passenger Car Association.

The U.S. automaker also notched a 15.5% month-on-month growth in deliveries of its China-made Model 3 and Model Y vehicles.

However, deliveries fell for the second consecutive month when compared to last year, despite an increase in subsidies from the Chinese government to attract more consumers.

The performance also lags major Chinese rivals by a significant margin. Chinese rival BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) set another sales record by shipping 506,804 new energy vehicles in November, representing a 67.87% Y/Y growth.

Other local EV competitors, including NIO (NIO), XPeng (XPEV) and Li Auto (LI), also reported higher sales on an annual basis.

Tesla (TSLA) and BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) have been offering extra incentives to local customers in a bid to help meet year-end sales targets. Elon Musk’s electric vehicle maker will have to sell at least 515,000 EVs globally in the final quarter of the year to achieve its projected growth in annual sales volume.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were down 1.37% premarket on Tuesday, but up ~44% year-to-date.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures tread cautiously with labor data on tap
data.description:
Stock index futures were almost flat on Tuesday, as investors turn their attention to a key labor report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX), Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND), and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.22%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat to 4.19%.

Major market averages closed out Monday’s session in a mixed fashion as the financial community digested the latest comments from President-elect Donald Trump as he threatened to implement more trade tariffs.

"Markets actually put in a decent performance yesterday, with the S&P 500 moving up to yet another record high. One supportive factor was an upside surprise in the ISM manufacturing for November," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

That equity rally was led by further strength among big tech stocks, and the Nasdaq and Magnificent 7 moved up to record highs of their own, Reid added.

The October Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS) report will land during market hours. It is one of the first of a few labor data expected in the week. Economists forecast a rise to 7.490M.

"The survey response rate is so low the numbers would not be published elsewhere. Recent variations in vacancies represented shifts between internal and external advertised positions," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Vacancies may be in focus if President-elect Donald Trump pursues deportations that create labor market bottlenecks, Donovan added.

data.title: Cencora prices $1.8B debt offering
data.description:
• Cencora (NYSE:COR) has priced an offering of $1.8B aggregate principal amount of senior notes.
  • The offering comprises $500M aggregate principal amount of Cencora’s 4.625% Senior Notes due Dec. 15, 2027; $600M of its 4.850% Senior Notes due Dec. 15, 2029; and $700M of its 5.150% Senior Notes due Feb. 15, 2035.
  • Cencora (COR) plans to use the net proceeds from the offering to finance the acquisition of the majority of Retina Consultants of America and related fees and expenses, and for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 9, 2024, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • COR shares were up 1.69% premarket to $254.10 on Tuesday.
